holographic_bound.note: holographic bound constrains both classical bits and qubits equally

quantum_simulation.approach: Hamiltonian simulation, d=2 per Planck site (minimal qubit model)

tensor_network_compression.1D_area_law: poly(chi) parameters, feasible for small chi
tensor_network_compression.3D_volume_law: S(A) ~ vol(A) — no polynomial compression possible
tensor_network_compression.observable_universe: 3D volume law — quantum simulation does NOT compress

superposition_economy_failure: Born rule collapses state to single classical outcome
Preparing arbitrary N-qubit state needs 2^N classical amplitudes
Each unitary step costs O(N) gate operations

minimum_faithful_resolution.interpretation: A simulator using all information in the observable universe can faithfully simulate down to ~4.7e-15 m (femtometer scale). Sub-femtometer structure — classical or quantum — cannot be simulated within the holographic budget.

summary.combined_with_lv_bounds: LIV constraints require simulator cells <= l_P. But Planck-cell simulation (classical or quantum) exceeds holographic bound. Simulation hypothesis is informationally self-defeating.
